source_display: AI as Brain Crutches for ADHD
evidence: Vrijen shared his personal interest in building AI tools as 'brain crutches' for ADHD—filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching

Vrijen shared advice from his edtech experience: avoid the 'death by a thousand cuts' of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is
He defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: 'you know it when you see it'—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly
Chris expressed reluctance around social media due to authenticity concerns and discomfort with inauthentic performance, especially given his work with children and parents
Vrijen outlined a spectrum of community approaches: one-to-many broadcasting → platforms with comment sections → private user research groups → full communities
Chris revealed having 65,000 active email subscribers built over years—a significant distribution asset for customer acquisition
Vrijen described the first year as characterized by face-plants and misplaced trust in technical partners

Chris cited a moral obligation not to burden teachers/coaches with costs when employers could pay. The strategy emphasizes organizational adoption for cohesion—getting everyone aligned on executive function support
The core concept is a 'marketplace of all possible futures' - allowing users to 'jump into' career simulations mapped to real jobs and companies

source_display: Fail Safely and Restart Career Simulation
evidence: His product enables users to fail safely and restart at the click of a button, removing consequences from learning
Max used the metaphor of a 'neopet' - anytime you train or deploy your AI requires 'energy,' which is where they monetize through usage-based pricing
Maximilian emphasized that his product creates portfolios first and assessments second, acknowledging that assessments are difficult to execute effectively
He criticized tools like HackerRank for not grounding results in actual business value - passing an algorithm assessment doesn't prove someone can deliver value to the company
Maximilian argued these roles are 'done' in the current AI-enabled landscape, with many PM positions facing similar elimination unless they manage P&L and have product experience
He noted that visible links between individual work and the product/service being sold are increasingly essential in 2026... engineers more than one step removed from the product were most vulnerable
Vrijen observed that the market for novel hiring signals is small because snake oil assessment companies have burned goodwill in the past
Vrijen used the example of parents obsessing over SATs because numbers are concrete and discrete, but noted this flattens humans to two axes inappropriately
The same person could be a generational hire for one role and a disaster for another, demonstrating context-dependent value
Vrijen described a self-fulfilling prophecy where increased hassle leads to corner-cutting, which then worsens outcomes and creates more hassle
He introduced context engineering as the foundational practice: not prompting the AI directly, but cultivating the context it uses to generate desired outputs
Vrijen outlined three strategic levels of context engineering: Conversation (tactical), Environment (persistent context), Pipeline (external data integration)
Vrijen argued the core issue isn't a technology or tactics problem but a mental model problem—users collect techniques without understanding how they integrate into effective systems
Ask the AI to create an evaluation rubric, fill it out, assign scores, and iterate output until achieving the highest level
Expansive generation (expand option set) vs. critical tightening (attack, break down, shore up)
Only 3,300 LinkedIn connections versus 45,000 imported contacts from a 2015 data import, revealing two largely disjoint professional networks
Claude identified 23 high-value DMs from the last six months that Logan had accidentally ghosted, including collaboration opportunities and podcast invitations
Documented 1,800% increase in inbound connections when increasing posting frequency, strong evidence for visibility strategies
Inbound versus outbound connection ratio as a measure of professional standing
embrace the 'unhobbling' concept from the Anthropic/Claude team—recognizing the AI is capable of more than you initially realize

source_display: Plan Files as Future of Coding
evidence: He emphasized the importance of intentional prompting—documenting AI conversations in plan files that mix prose and code in stepwise fashion. He positioned this as the future of coding
Ben highlighted Zo's integration capabilities: text/email workflows enabling parallel work during passive moments (airport lines, cab rides)
consistently ask 'what else?' and 'delight me'
An AI productivity workshop presented by Careerspan (Vrijen Attawar and Logan Currie) and Zo Computer (Ben Guo)... Vrijen as a power Zo user
An AI productivity workshop presented by Careerspan (Vrijen Attawar and Logan Currie)
